requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL persist unblock-capable actor control signals durably and replay or re-deliver them during session recovery.

case.given: A tool, LLM, child, human, or cancel event has been persisted as a durable control signal
case.then: The process crashes before the matching resume is applied
The runtime SHALL re-deliver or reduce the durable signal into the actor mailbox or cooperative exec state
The owning fiber SHALL become schedulable if the signal satisfies its wait condition

case.given: A durable control signal has been enqueued into an actor mailbox
case.then: The process crashes before transcript or projection persistence completes
The control signal SHALL remain available for actor execution
Transcript projection SHALL be rebuilt or repaired as a read model

case.given: The same durable control signal is encountered more than once during recovery
case.then: The runtime SHALL de-duplicate by event id, op id, fiber id, and tool call id as applicable
The actor SHALL NOT apply the same completion twice

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL reject, repair, or explicitly diagnose suspended fiber snapshots that have no recoverable wait condition or pending control signal.

case.given: A fiber snapshot would be saved as suspended with generic external waiting
case.then: The actor has no pending mailbox entries, no cooperative exec state, no durable pending control signal, and no terminal condition
case.when: The runtime attempts to save or recover the snapshot
case.then: The runtime SHALL NOT silently persist or accept the state as a healthy blocked fiber
The runtime SHALL either mark it recoverable-failed with diagnostics or rehydrate it to a deterministic ready state when a safe continuation rule exists

case.given: The transcript or conversation history ends with a tool result
case.then: The runtime MAY use the transcript tail as a conservative repair hint
The runtime SHALL prefer durable control signals, actor mailboxes, and cooperative exec state as the primary control truth

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL keep the VM snapshot as a bounded durable subset and SHALL NOT persist complete AI message history, provider-private content, or large tool outputs in `runtime_state/vm.json`.

case.given: An actor turn produces assistant messages, reasoning content, tool calls, and tool results
case.then: The VM snapshot SHALL NOT contain complete `llm_done.msg`, `tool_done.output`, `tool_done.outputText`, `reasoning_content`, or complete tool schemas
The message content SHALL remain recoverable from actor transcript, conversation history, or a referenced artifact store

case.given: A session runs many LLM/tool cycles and all corresponding control signals have been consumed
case.when: The runtime writes repeated VM snapshots
case.then: The serialized VM snapshot size SHALL remain bounded by session control state, not by historical LLM/tool content volume
Consumed control signal records SHALL be compacted or checkpointed into small idempotency tombstones

case.given: A legacy session contains full LLM/tool payloads inside `sessionState.controlSignals.events`
case.when: The runtime recovers and saves the session with the new snapshot writer
case.then: The runtime SHALL preserve recoverability
The next saved VM snapshot SHALL normalize or migrate those control signals to the bounded durable format

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL persist durable control signal payloads as typed metadata or payload references rather than unbounded mailbox payload copies.

case.given: An async LLM or tool operation completes with a large result
case.when: The runtime emits the durable control signal that wakes the owning fiber
case.then: The signal SHALL store op id, tool call id, mailbox kind, signal kind, priority, created time, and a small payload reference or digest
The full completion payload SHALL be delivered through the actor mailbox and persisted by the appropriate actor mailbox, transcript, conversation, or artifact surface

case.given: A mailbox payload is not yet consumed at snapshot time
case.then: The actor mailbox snapshot SHALL retain enough payload data to continue mailbox drain after recovery
The VM control signal snapshot SHALL NOT duplicate that payload as a second content truth source

case.given: Derived indexes or projection files exist for member, detached actor, coordination, conversation, or UI views
case.then: Those projections SHALL either be rebuildable from VM, actor, fiber, transcript, conversation, and artifact truth sources or be explicitly modeled as durable state
They SHALL NOT silently become the only source of truth for recoverable state

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL provide guard tests or diagnostics that prevent VM snapshot boundary regressions.

case.given: A test runtime produces assistant reasoning, tool output, MCP output, and consumed control signals
case.then: The test SHALL fail if `vm.json` contains provider-private fields such as `reasoning_content` or full `tool_done.outputText` payloads

case.given: A fixture produces repeated consumed LLM/tool completion signals
case.when: The test compares VM snapshot size before and after additional consumed history
case.then: The test SHALL fail if VM snapshot growth is proportional to historical message or tool payload bytes

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL persist questionnaire state as first-class rows in `runtime_state/questionnaires.xnl` and SHALL NOT persist questionnaire request payloads inside actor snapshots.

case.given: A runtime has pending or answered questionnaires
case.then: The snapshot SHALL write `runtime_state/questionnaires.xnl` as repeated `QuestionnaireRow` XNL data nodes without an outer wrapper node
The writer SHALL use xnl-core parsing and stringifying APIs rather than XML syntax or ad hoc string concatenation

case.given: An actor owns a pending questionnaire
case.when: The runtime writes actor snapshot files
case.then: The actor metadata, state, and mailbox snapshot files SHALL NOT store `pendingQuestionnaires` as durable questionnaire truth
The questionnaire SHALL remain recoverable from `runtime_state/questionnaires.xnl`

case.given: A session is recovered from snapshot files
case.then: The runtime SHALL rebuild the actor surface pending questionnaire list from those rows
The TUI SHALL show pending questionnaires when loading the session

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL recover and schedule actor mailbox wake work even when the corresponding durable control signal is present only as a consumed tombstone.

suite.name: Consumed signal with pending mailbox payload

case.given: `runtime_state/vm.json` contains a consumed `async_completed` control-signal tombstone for op id `X`
case.then: The actor mailbox snapshot still contains `asyncCompletion` payload for op id `X`
The actor mailbox payload SHALL remain the recoverable execution truth
The owning fiber SHALL become schedulable without requiring a pending durable signal event

case.given: A recovered actor has pending wake mailbox work and no serialized pending resume
case.then: The runtime SHALL rebuild the equivalent schedulable state from actor mailbox plus cooperative exec state
The runtime SHALL NOT require transcript-tail inference to resume the fiber

case.given: A wake message was durably delivered to an actor mailbox before process exit
case.then: The explicit `pendingResumes` scheduler set was lost or absent
The pending actor mailbox entry SHALL be sufficient to re-schedule the fiber

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL reject, repair, or explicitly diagnose snapshots that persist a cooperative async wait as an apparently idle ready fiber.

case.given: A snapshot would persist a fiber as `ready` with no waiting reason
case.then: The cooperative exec state is `wait_llm` with inflight work
case.when: The snapshot is saved or recovered
case.then: The runtime SHALL NOT silently accept it as ordinary idle state
The runtime SHALL repair it to a schedulable or typed waiting state and emit diagnostics when repair is applied

case.given: A snapshot would persist a fiber as `ready` with cooperative exec state `wait_tool` and inflight work
case.when: The snapshot is saved or recovered
case.then: The runtime SHALL preserve a deterministic continuation path for the pending tool result

case.given: A recovery fixture has no pending durable control signal events
case.then: It has consumed wake tombstones, pending actor mailbox payloads, and cooperative wait state
The test SHALL prove that mailbox-owned payloads still drive recovery and execution

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL save runtime snapshots only at recoverable safepoints or preserve the previous known-good snapshot when the current runtime state is not a safepoint.

case.given: An actor history contains an assistant tool call that requires tool execution
case.then: The owning fiber cooperative exec state is `start_tool`
No durable tool operation or matching tool result exists for that tool call
case.when: The runtime attempts to save a snapshot
case.then: The snapshot writer SHALL NOT persist that state as the latest recoverable snapshot
The runtime SHALL either advance to a safepoint or skip the save while preserving the previous known-good snapshot

case.given: An actor history contains an assistant tool call without a matching tool result
case.then: The snapshot SHALL contain recoverable proof that the matching tool operation has started or is waiting for completion
Recovery SHALL be able to continue without relying on transcript-tail inference

case.given: A snapshot save has completed successfully
case.when: The process exits immediately after the save and the session is recovered
case.then: The recovered runtime SHALL be able to continue or remain in a typed external wait state
The recovered message history SHALL NOT contain unmatched assistant tool calls unless a durable operation can produce the matching tool result

case.given: The runtime attempts to save while mandatory continuations remain unconsumed
case.when: The save is skipped or delayed because the state is not a safepoint
case.then: The runtime SHALL emit diagnostics describing the blocking fiber, phase, and reason
The diagnostics SHALL NOT expose provider-private message content or large tool payloads

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL store every append-only session record stream as parser-valid top-level XNL records appended with filesystem append operations, and SHALL keep replace-written state files bounded.

case.given: The implementation writes or reads runtime XNL persistence records covered by this requirement
case.when: The implementation serializes, appends, or reads those records
case.then: The implementation SHALL use shared project-level XNL formatter, append helper, and read helper where practical
The formatter SHALL follow xnl-core-compatible AST and syntax rules
The append helper SHALL write one complete top-level record plus newline per append
The read helper SHALL be built on XNL-provided parser/helper functions rather than reimplementing an XNL parser
The read helper SHALL parse multi-root append streams and reject root-wrapper growing history for append-only streams

case.given: A session file records historical facts, audit facts, replay facts, diagnostics, message history, prompt materialization history, orchestration events, or runtime-control evidence
case.when: The component persists the fact to an XNL file
case.then: The component SHALL append one complete top-level XNL element per record
The component SHALL NOT rewrite a growing root wrapper, JSON array, JSONL stream, or plain text transcript as the authoritative append-only format

case.given: An append-only XNL record has exactly one event subject, payload, payload reference, or structured result
case.then: A pure key-value subject SHALL be represented in the top-level node's custom attribute block
A single subject that has its own tag, metadata, attributes, or text marker semantics SHALL be represented as a named child node in `()`
The writer SHALL use `[]` body only when the record has multiple peer child nodes, ordered blocks, or mixed text/data children

case.given: A session file stores bounded current state such as a manifest, index, head, cursor, checkpoint marker, or projection metadata
case.then: The component MAY use replace-written JSON or replace-written XNL only if the file does not contain historical record bodies
The file SHALL remain bounded by current state rather than growing with session event count

case.given: An XNL file is currently written by full replacement
case.when: The file's rows represent lifecycle history or recovery/audit evidence rather than bounded current state
case.then: The implementation SHALL convert it to an append-only XNL stream or split historical facts into a dedicated append-only XNL stream

case.given: The runtime persists actor conversation history records
case.then: The record SHALL be appended to `conversation/history.xnl` as a top-level XNL record
`conversation/history-generations/*.json` SHALL NOT be used as the authoritative format for new or upgraded sessions

case.given: The runtime persists prompt materialization records that are retained for recovery, audit, or debugging
case.then: The record SHALL be appended to `conversation/prompts.xnl` as a top-level XNL record
Prompt caches that are rebuildable SHALL be explicitly non-authoritative

case.given: The runtime persists actor-originated conversation history
case.when: The record is saved in a new or upgraded session
case.then: The actor source SHALL be represented in `conversation/history.xnl` generation and message metadata
The runtime SHALL NOT create or append to `actors/&lt;actor-dir&gt;/transcript.xnl` as an independent history file
`transcript.txt` and legacy `transcript.xnl` files SHALL NOT be used as recovery or append-only history sources for new or upgraded sessions

case.given: The runtime-control engine records effect lifecycle evidence
case.then: The evidence SHALL be appended to `runtime-control/effects.xnl` as a top-level XNL record with monotonic sequence metadata
`runtime-control/effects.jsonl` SHALL NOT be used for new or upgraded sessions

case.given: The runtime records orchestration history
case.then: The event SHALL be appended to `logs/orchestration_history.xnl` as a top-level XNL record
`logs/orchestration_history.jsonl` SHALL NOT be used for new or upgraded sessions

case.given: The runtime records ingress or diagnostic event facts
case.then: The event SHALL be appended to `logs/ingress.xnl` or `logs/diagnostics.xnl` as a top-level XNL record
Any head JSON for those logs SHALL contain only bounded cursor, count, id, timestamp, or sequence state

case.given: The runtime writes a record to `conversation/history.xnl`
case.then: The top-level XNL node SHALL represent one message, not a generation wrapper
The message metadata SHALL include actor source fields such as `actorKey` and `actorId`

case.given: A history message contains multiple blocks
case.then: The message body SHALL contain the block nodes in order
`Think` and `Content` blocks SHALL be XNL text nodes without a `mime` metadata field
`Think` and `Content` text markers SHALL be generated ULIDs
Non-text blocks such as tool calls, tool results, artifact refs, and questionnaire refs SHALL be XNL data nodes

case.given: A history message is persisted to `conversation/history.xnl`, including runtime commits, compaction writes, legacy history-generation migration, and transcript-only legacy session bootstrap
case.then: The writer SHALL NOT persist legacy transcript-shaped `sourceRecords` payload blobs or `transcriptPath` fields in `HistoryMessage` attributes
The message text SHALL live only in the block children rather than being duplicated as attribute payload strings
Readers MAY keep accepting `sourceRecords` attributes from records written before this rule for tool-call-id recovery and user-input history compatibility

case.given: The history writer serializes message and block nodes
case.then: The formatter SHALL keep the node name, metadata fields, and attribute block on the same opening line when an attribute block exists
The formatter SHALL be based on xnl-core compatible syntax or AST rules rather than ad hoc XML string concatenation

case.given: The runtime writes a record to `conversation/prompts.xnl`
case.then: The top-level XNL node SHALL represent one `PromptGeneration` record
The prompt generation metadata SHALL include `id`, `sessionId`, `actorKey`, `actorId`, `reason`, `sealed`, `createdAt`, and `updatedAt` when available

case.given: A prompt generation has basis refs, transforms, and materialized context
case.then: The body SHALL contain structured second-layer nodes such as `Basis`, `BasisRef`, `Transform`, and `MaterializedContext`
`MaterializedContext` SHALL be an XNL text node with a generated ULID marker
`Basis`, `BasisRef`, and `Transform` SHALL be XNL data nodes

case.given: A prompt materialization is persisted
case.then: The record SHALL explicitly state whether it is recoverable audit truth or a rebuildable non-authoritative cache
Rebuildable prompt caches SHALL NOT be used as recovery fact sources

case.given: The runtime-control engine writes a record to `runtime-control/effects.xnl`
case.then: The top-level XNL node SHALL represent one `RuntimeEffectEvent` lifecycle event
The top-level metadata SHALL include `sequence`, `kind`, `effectKind`, `effectId`, and `handlerKey`
The implementation SHALL NOT wrap the full lifecycle event object in a generic nested `event` data node

case.given: An effect lifecycle event has kind `request`, `waiting`, `result`, or `failed`
case.then: The event-specific subject SHALL be stored as a named child node in `()`, such as `Request`, `Wait`, `Result`, or `Error`
The writer SHALL NOT use a `[]` body solely to wrap a single event-specific subject
Small structured subjects SHALL be expanded directly into the event-specific child node attributes rather than wrapped as `{ payload = ... }` by default
The writer MAY use a `payload` field only when that field has a real sibling at the same level and the schema documents the sibling semantics
Large payloads, text bodies, provider outputs, or tool outputs SHALL be stored as artifact refs, digests, or bounded summaries rather than top-level metadata

case.given: The runtime writes a record to `logs/ingress.xnl`
case.then: The top-level XNL node SHALL represent the ingress category directly rather than a generic `IngressEvent` wrapper
The writer SHALL NOT use `IngressEvent` containing `Data` containing `{ payload = ... }` for new records

case.given: An ingress event contains think or content text
case.then: The event SHALL be written as a typed XNL text node such as `Think` or `Content`
The human-readable text SHALL be the text node body, not JSON inside a payload field

case.given: An ingress event contains tool, control, or other structured data
case.then: The event SHALL use a typed top-level data node with metadata and direct attributes when the subject is a pure key-value structure
The event MAY use `()` only when a single child subject has its own tag, metadata, attributes, or text-marker semantics

case.given: The runtime writes a record to `logs/orchestration_history.xnl`
case.then: The top-level XNL node SHALL represent one `OrchestrationEvent`
The top-level metadata SHALL include `version`, `sequence`, `observedAt`, `stream`, and `kind`
The implementation SHALL NOT wrap the full event object in a generic nested `event` data node

case.given: An orchestration event has one payload subject
case.then: Small structured payloads SHALL be stored as a `Payload` child node in `()`
Large hook reports, long text, or complex diagnostic objects SHALL be stored as `PayloadRef` child nodes in `()` with artifact refs, digests, or bounded summaries

case.given: `runtime_state/questionnaires.xnl` stores only current questionnaire rows for recovery of pending or answered questionnaire state
case.then: The file MAY remain a replace-written bounded XNL table of `QuestionnaireRow` nodes
The implementation SHALL prove that the table does not grow as an audit log of questionnaire lifecycle events
`QuestionnaireRow` SHALL use `[]` for multiple typed children such as `Request`, `Result`, and `Metadata`

case.given: The runtime needs questionnaire lifecycle audit or replay history
case.then: The facts SHALL be stored in a dedicated append-only XNL stream rather than hidden inside the replace-written questionnaire state table

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL make XNL conversation history the upgraded session's authoritative message history format.

case.given: An upgraded session has conversation history records
case.when: The session is saved or recovered after upgrade
case.then: The session SHALL use XNL append-only conversation history records instead of `conversation/history-generations/*.json` as the authoritative history source

case.given: A legacy session contains actor `transcript.txt` files
case.when: The session is upgraded to the new append-only storage model
case.then: The upgrade SHALL migrate transcript facts into XNL record streams
The upgraded session SHALL NOT retain `transcript.txt` as a recovery or append-only history source

case.given: Conversation indexes or heads are written after upgrade
case.when: The system updates visible heads, active actor selection, or generation cursors
case.then: The JSON index/head files SHALL only point to XNL stream positions or generation ids and SHALL NOT store growing message bodies

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL store runtime-control evidence and orchestration history as XNL append-only streams.

case.given: The runtime-control engine records an effect lifecycle event
case.then: The event SHALL be appended to an XNL effect evidence stream
The system SHALL NOT use `runtime-control/effects.jsonl` for new upgraded sessions

case.given: The runtime records orchestration history
case.then: The event SHALL be appended to an XNL orchestration stream
The system SHALL NOT use `logs/orchestration_history.jsonl` for new upgraded sessions

case.given: The runtime updates diagnostic or ingress log heads
case.when: The head JSON file is written
case.then: The head SHALL contain cursor state such as count, sequence, id, and timestamp only
The head SHALL NOT embed the full last event payload

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL classify prompt materialization data as either XNL audit records or rebuildable cache, and SHALL NOT persist prompt history as many authoritative JSON generation files.

case.given: A prompt materialization must be retained for recovery, audit, or debugging
case.then: The system SHALL append it as an XNL prompt record

case.given: A prompt materialization is only a rebuildable cache
case.then: The cache SHALL be explicitly marked as non-authoritative and SHALL NOT be used as a recovery fact source

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL source snapshot safepoint decisions from the AI runtime control layer rather than from the persistence writer.

case.given: The snapshot writer is asked to save runtime state
case.when: It checks whether the runtime is at a safepoint
case.then: The writer SHALL call the AI runtime control layer for the barrier result
The writer SHALL only persist or skip according to that result

requirement.statement: 对话恢复 SHALL 以会话文件（history、prompts、session.index）为唯一来源；SHALL NOT 读取 actor transcript（该形态已删除）；transcript-only 旧 session SHALL 被显式拒绝而非静默降级。

case.given: 恢复流程选择对话来源
case.when: 会话文件缺失或不完整
case.then: 恢复 SHALL 失败并返回显式错误
SHALL NOT 回退到 transcript 或其它部分事实源
